EncyclopediaEntry.Description: Fleets are basically groups of ships that work or travel together.
Fleets are basically groups of ships that work or travel together.

Abregado, site of the Abregado-rae Spaceport, is controlled by an oppressive government which has kept the peace and improved the local spaceport at the expense of its citizens. It has become one of the major manufacturing centers of the Abrion sector. The natives of Abregado are called Gados.
The Adega system was once the site of an important ancient Jedi stronghold. The system is the main source of Ilum crystals, also called Adegan crystals, which are used in Jedi lightsabers.

Agrilat contains a crystal swamp where an illegal and dangerous swoop race between two young pilots, Dengar and Han Solo, was once held. Dengar was injured during the course of the race and now has burning hatred for Solo.
Akrit'tar is a barren and lifeless world which serves as one of the galaxy's many penal colonies.

The Algarian system is well known for its huge and complex governing bureaucracy. No other information is available on this system without submission of a Form AA-23-7022 and a 150 credit processing fee to the Algarian Bureau of Planetary Information and Tourism.
The homeworld of a dangerous carnivore called the Ketrann.
Allyuen is a remote desert world much like Tatooine. It was one of the possible locations the Empire suspected that the Rebel Alliance might be based after the Battle of Yavin.
Altarrn is the homeworld of a rare animal known as spidery nightgliders. These animals are considered premium pets and are sold at Sabodor's pet shop on Etti IV.

The desert planet Ambria is located in the heart of the Xappyh sector. Animal life on Ambria includes herds of staga, small lizards called neeks, and strange, evil creatures from Lake Natth, a place strong with the dark side of the Force. Four thousand years ago, Ambria was the home of Thon, a great Jedi Master.
Amorris is a small relatively uninteresting world on the Rim that is rumored to be the base of the notorious Cavrilhu pirate gang.
Anchoron was the site where Senator Garm Bel Iblis was believed killed.

A watery planet with very few land masses, Ando is the homeworld of the belligerent, walrus-faced Aqualish. The finned Aqualish called Aquala prefer to live on floating raft-cities and large sailing ships, while the fingered Aqualish called Quara inhabit the larger islands. Ando had a sister planet in the same system, but it was blasted uninhabitable by an Aqualish war soon after the species discovered space travel.
Annaj is a large gas giant which is rumored to serve as a staging area for the Imperial fleet prior to operations in the Moddell sector.
One of the three habitable worlds in the Anoat system, the planet Anoat has served as a colony world for the pig-like Ugnaughts.
Arkania is a world covered with tundra and rich diamond mines. Four thousand years ago, the Jedi Master Arca established a Jedi training outpost in the wilderness of Arkania.
Averam is one of the few core systems that is rumored to openly sympathize with small groups of Rebel Alliance supporters.
Azbian is a world of sweeping plains and grasslands. Most of the planet is devoted to agriculture and the grazing of the indigenous, eight-legged herd animals.

Bakura is a rich, green and blue planet with several moons located on the isolated edge of the Rim Systems. Bakura's primary exports include strategic metals, repulsorlift components, and an addictive fruit called namana. The planet's inhabitants tend to be prejudiced against non-human species and especially dislike droids.
Balfron is a small terrestrial planet on the outer rim. Balfron sports several high stakes casinos where planets can be won or lost in a game of sabacc.
Balmorra is a factory world located at the Galactic Core. The Balmorrans are one of the primary manufacturers of weapons for the Imperial army, and were the builders of the AT-ST walker.
Basilisk is a desolate world that was once the site of the Battle of Basilisk, where the Jedi Master Sidrona Diath was killed by the Sith.

Berchest is major tourist destination and home of the City of Glowing Crystal. The city is carved entirely from one massive crystal, created over the centuries from the salt deposits of the adjacent Leefari Sea.

A many-mooned gaseous system, Bespin is home to several mining operations, including Tibannopolis and Cloud City, that draw valuable tibanna gas from below the cloud layers. Cloud City is staffed by a large population of Ugnaughts from the nearby Anoat system, and is not a part of the Mining Guild or the Empire. Bespin's fauna includes birdlike rawwks and airborne algae sacks.

The Bilbringi system hosts the Imperial Shipyards which are a mass of dockyard platforms, partially-built ships, and construction vehicles. Most of the Imperial Navy was crafted at the Bilbringi shipyards, many of which have been destoyed in fighting with the Alliance.

Bimmisaari is the homeworld of a species of small, half-furred, yellow-clad aliens called Bimms. Bimmisaari is known for its unusual moving trees, and a three-level, two-hundred year old marketplace. All weapons of violence are banned from the cities of Bimmisaari.
Boordii is a temperate world whose central source of income seems to be from smugglers transporting contraband offworld.
Bortras is a tranquil world of lush valleys and fertile landscapes. It is also known as the birth world of famed Jedi Master Jorus C'baoth.

Bothawui is the homeworld of the Bothans. Bothans are known for being master politicians and spies, craving intrigue and subterfuge. It is suspected that Bothan spies liberated the plans for the Death Star and passed them on to the Alliance.

Bpfassh is a double planet with a complicated system of moons located near Praesitlyn and Sluis Van in the Sluis sector. During the Clone Wars some Bpfasshi Dark Jedi were responsible for a rampage of terror and destruction throughout the sector. A Jedi task force lead by Jorus C'baoth was sent to stop them and quell the insurrection in the system. To this day, the Bpfasshi dislike all Jedi.

Byss, the homeworld of the one-eyed Abyssin, travels in an unusual figure-eight orbit between the binary stars of Byss and Abyss. It is a hot arid planet, and temperatures reach their apex when the planet is orbiting directly between both stars. Most plant life on Byss utilize extensive tap-roots to extract underground water. Byss is largely ignored in galactic trade, its one substantial export being the Abyssin themselves as slaves.

A tranquil world near the galactic core, Caprionril was the site of the infamous assassin droid tragedy in which a droid slaughtered 20,000 humanoids and aliens at a swoop arena in order to assassinate Governor Amel Bakli.
Cardooine is the source of fragrant Fijisi wood. Sections of the Imperial Palace on Coruscant are constructed of Fijisi wood from Cardooine.

Carida is a large, high-gravity world with a wide variety of terrain. Carida is the location of the most important Imperial military training center. Stormtrooper candidates undergo harsh training in the varied environments to prepare themselves for action on a variety of worlds. Admiral Daala attended the Caridan academy before her appointment to Tarkin's staff.
The homeworld of a feline alien species. The Jedi Knights Sylvar and Crado, who trained under Vodo-Siosk Baas, were natives of Cathar.
A rocky, volcanic world with a semi-breathable atmosphere, Chalcedon is the hub in the galactic slave trade.
Chandrila is a lush world covered with woods and trees. Chandrila is also the homeworld of Alliance leader Mon Mothma.
Charmath is the home to one of the most complete historical archives in the galaxy and the site of the famous University of Charmath.

Chazwa's location makes it an ideal shipping center for all sorts of goods. Heavy freight traffic is a common sight in the system, and the abundance of valuable merchandise moving through the system lends itself to a strong influence by smugglers.

The once beautiful world of Chrondre has been heavily damaged by the ongoing war. Chrondre was colonized generations ago and believed strongly in peaceful existence, until it was forcibly conquered by Imperial forces.

Clak'dor VII is a small planet orbiting the large white star Colu and the homeworld of the peaceful, highly-evolved Bith. Clak'dor VII was once a lush world with incredibly advanced technologies, but is currently an ecological wasteland due to a local war using biological weapons. Since the disaster, Clak'dor VII is unable to produce either the basic needs for its citizens or goods for export. Thus many Bith sell their intellectual abilities for employment as consultants.
Commenor, deep within the Corellian sector, is known for its crysopaz gemstones and local brandy.

Cona, the homeworld of the triangle-headed Arcona, is a hot, dense world orbiting the blue giant Teke Ro. Due to Cona's lack of axial tilt and temperature-distributing air currents, the climate is effectively the same everywhere on the planet's surface.

Corellia has a rich history dating back before the time of the Old Republic. It is suspected that Corellia and its four sister planets were not always in the system, but were moved there by some unknown race. Huge repulsorlifts and hyperdrives beneath the surface of the planets lend substance to this theory. Corellia is best known for her vast shipyards and excellent pilots.
One of the older and more established worlds of the Corellian sector, Corfai has been a center for trade and commerce for generations.

Corsin is a beautiful world known for its love of life until it was subjugated by Imperial troops. Alliance historian Voren Na'al witnessed the start of the military occupation while covering the Corsin swoop races.
Corstris is a heavily forested planet on the outer Rim. Imperial intelligence once suspected it to be the location of a hidden Alliance base.

Coruscant has been the governmental capital of the galaxy for generations. The planet's entire surface is covered by an enormous, multileveled city, called Imperial City, whose foundations have been in place since the beginning of the Old Republic, over a thousand generations ago. The pyramidal Imperial Palace, an impregnable fortress and home to the Emperor, is constructed of gray-green rock and sparkling crystals and is taller than all other structures including the Senate building. The lowest, darkest levels of Imperial City were abandoned long ago and are now home to discarded equipment, wrecked starships, and nameless subhumans moving through the shadows.

A planet with a long history of extreme violence, Culroon III was largely ignored by the Old Republic. Due to their constant warring, the Culroon natives never developed space travel. When the Empire decided to construct a garrison on Culroon III, the Imperial general in charge of the operation agreed to stage a mock surrender of the Culroon people by their leader. When it turned out to be an ambush by the Culroon, the Imperial staff was rescued by an AT-AT commanded by then Lieutenant Veers.

Da Soocha, sometimes called Gla Soocha, is covered almost entirely by water. It has served as an evacuation site for the Mon Calamari during Imperial attacks on their world. Its fifth moon is called the Pinnacle Moon and has been suspected to possibly house an Alliance outpost.
Little is known of the planet Daltar except that it once served as a listening outpost for the Alliance monitoring Imperial fleets. It was later discovered and eliminated by Lord Vader.

Dantooine is a lovely blue and brown colored planet with no industrial settlements or advanced technology far removed from most galactic traffic. Its surface is covered with savannas of lavender grasses and blba trees. Four thousand years ago, the Jedi Master Vodo-Siosk Baas established a Jedi training center among Dantooine's ruins. Many centuries later, Dantooine for awhile served as an Alliance base, but was evacuated sometime after the Death Star became operational.

Dar'Or is recorded in Imperial charts as OM973. The planet is a low-gravity, forested world, which orbits an orange sun and is home to the intelligent flying mammals called ri'dar. Dar'Or has been declared an Imperial Species Preservation Zone, but some smugglers have bypassed the travel restrictions.

Dathomir is a low-gravity world with three continents, a wide ocean, and four small moons. Centuries ago, the Jedi banished one of their own to Dathomir who began training the inhabitants in the use of the Force. Some time later the Jedi spaceborne academy Chu'unthor crashed on Dathomir and the Jedi force lead by Yoda sent to recover it was repulsed by the Witches of Dathomir. The witches were those female inhabitants who had learned to use the Force.
Davnar is a little known world in the Kanchen sector that is home to a bipedal, scaly, long-snouted alien species.
Delaya was the sister world of the planet Alderaan. Delaya served as an evacuation site for some of the few who escaped Alderaan before its destruction by the Death Star.
Denab was the site where the Imperial Fourth Attack Squadron, composed of Victory-class Star Destroyers, was defeated by the Alliance.
Derra IV has long been an outpost and storage dump for the Rebel Alliance. The planet has little of value and has been relatively overlooked by the Empire, making it ideal for Alliance operations.
Despayre is a penal colony located at the distant rim of the galaxy. Because of its remote location and plentiful labor force, Despayre was chosen as the construction site of the first Death Star.

Deyer is a colony world composed of floating raft cities, terraformed lakes, and abundant aquatic life. The colonists on Deyer had created a peaceful, democratic political system until they spoke out against the destruction of Alderaan. The Empire immediately responded with a brutal military takeover of Deyer.
Douglas III is a remote world with very little of interest. Located far from the galactic core, Douglas III has become the home to pirates and black market traders.

Drall is a pleasant, temperate planet with a lighter gravity than standard. As with other worlds in the Corellian system, Drall has a vast, subterranean planetary repulsorlift located near the equator. It was presumably used in ancient times to move the planet into its current position from an unknown location.

A terrestrial planet is the homeworld of the alien species also known as Duros which have been traveling space for thousands of years. The Duros system is filled with vast, orbiting cities and shipyards governed by a group of starship corporations. Political decisions are made by the stockholders of these corporations. The planet Duros is primarily uninhabited, and is covered with automated farms that help feed the inhabitants of the space cities.
Elrood is the site of a commercial colony that contains prefabricated room units from the factories of Sullust.

The forest moon of Endor, called the Sanctuary Moon, is a temperate, densely forested world. The moon's low axial tilt and the regular orbit of its gas giant primary helps ensure a comfortable climate. Endor is the home to many native species which include the primitive Ewoks.

Engira for some time now has basically served the Empire as an Imperial supply colony. Convoys of supplies are directed to Imperial garrisons and facilities throughout the galaxy, making Engira a prime target for pirates.

F'tral is a large water covered world orbiting the blue-white star F'laRen. It is home to the tentacled cephalopods known as the Iyra. The Iyra inhabit large undersea cities and have developed many valuable technologies, including corrosion-resistant alloys and gravity-field and inertial devices for starships.
A forested planet in the Atrivis sector, Fedje is host to many forms of exotic arboreal lifeforms including the rare folded ear boosqueak.

Fef is a moderately large planet orbiting an orange-yellow star and is the homeworld of the insectoid Fefze. The Fefze are able to digest all forms of carbon-based organic matter and due to the abundance of food available to the Fefze and their lack of dexterous appendages, they have not developed technologically and have few exports.

Filve is a simple world in the Dufilvan sector that has received a lot of punishment from Imperial forces, both from actual attacks on the planet and damage from battles occurring above it. Lately, it has been showing strong loyalties towards the Alliance.
Firro was the site of many deaths and atrocities during its brutal subjugation by the Empire. Lord Cuvir is the Imperial Governor of Firro.

Flax is covered by oceans but most of the surface land masses are desert, due to the high coastal mountain ranges which prevent atmospheric transference of moisture from the seas. Flax is also the home to the insectoid species called Flakax. After the planet was conquered by the Empire, the Flakax were declared slave workers and were put to work mining Flax's underground minerals.

Fornax is well known throughout the galaxy as being the home of the Fire Rings of Fornax. The rings are actually five large solar prominences that encircle the planet, attracted to it by its close proximity to the system's star. The Fire Rings are one of the natural wonders of the galaxy and not only attract tourists wanting to view their beauty, but herds of daredevil young pilots who risk their lives to fly through the Rings. One such pilot was a young Corellian named Han Solo.

Fwillsving is a planet near Honoghr and Kessel that is sometimes used by smugglers on the Kessel Run as a repair depot. Smuggling spice from the mines on Kessel has become increasingly dangerous due to Imperial intervention in the sector, thus making Fwillsving a reasonable hideaway for those seeking to avoid Imperial entanglements.
G'rho is a remote world on the Outer Rim that was apparently one of the first planets attacked by an alien species known as the Ssi-Ruuk.
Galpos II is a desolate world in the Abrion sector that is suspected to be a base of operations for several of the gangs of pirates in the sector.

Gamorr's varied terrain ranges from frozen tundra to deep forests, and it is considered quite pleasant by off-worlders. It is also the home to the pig-faced Gamorreans, who seem to care only for vicious, bloody warfare which is constantly occurring between rival clans. Because of their great strength and low intelligence, many Gamorreans are employed throughout the galaxy as guards, mercenaries, and enforcers.
Gandolo IV is a barren, rocky moon located in the Moddell sector. A group of Wookiee settlers have attempted to establish a colony on the moon.

Garban is a temperate world with high seasonal variance and home to the species called the Jenet. Years ago, the rapidly reproducing Jenet wiped out most of their natural predators on Garban, which led to overpopulation and the colonization of the other worlds. Jenet are quarrelsome and have a massive bureaucratic government whose job it is to remember everything they can about each Jenet citizen. After Garban was invaded by the Empire, the Jenet were forced to work as slaves in Garban's ore mines.
Geedon V was the site of a former base for pirates operating in the Sumitra sector. Once, it was single-handedly taken over by the famed gunman Gallandro, during one of his early exploits.
The grassy world of Generis for some time has been site of the Alliance's hidden Outer Rim Comm Center.
Gentes is the homeworld of the pig-like Ugnaughts. The Ugnaughts lived in primitive colonies on Gentes' volcanic surface until most left the planet to work at Cloud City of Bespin.

The site of the infamous Ghorman massacre, an early atrocity committed by the Empire when then Governor Tarkin purposefully landed his ship on top of a crowd of peaceful demonstrators. The massacre has been commemorated every year on its anniversary by those opposed to Emperor Palpatine's New Order. The Massacre was responsible for convincing Bail Organa of Alderaan to join the Alliance.
H'nemthe is a forest planet with three moons. This planet located in the Mayagil sector, is home to the species called H'nemthe.
Halowan is a barren planet in the Fakir Sector.
This system in the Abrion Sector has varied climates and can support various forms of humanoids.
Hishyim, a planet from the Abrion Sector, is covered by a vast desert.
Hoth is the sixth planet in a star system of the same name. It is a frozen world of wind, snow, and ice in which vehicles and machinery are affected adversely and must be modified.
Hozrel XI located in the Moddell sector is an almost barren rock. The little life that does inhabit the planet's surface are by far some of the most savage creatures in the galaxy.
Historically, the planet Intuci was raided by the war criminal Sonopo Bommoor. Bommoor's forces sacked the city of Bonaka Nueno, and massacred its citizens.

Ithor is a beautiful forest planet in the Calaron Sector. The planet is inhabited by beings called Ithorians, who live in great floating cities. The Ithorians rarely if ever set foot in the forests below their cities and consider these forests sacred.
Jerijador, located in the Calaron Sector, teems with many different kinds of life and is quite hospitable to humanoids.
This planet in the Orus sector seems to teem with life.
The planet is mostly ocean, with one small continent called Kalish and thousands of small islands. The colonists who came to this planet long ago live by fishing and sea farming.
Kabal is a neutral world and the site of the Conference of Uncommitted Worlds, which was held just after the destruction of the Death Star.
Kamparas is virtually covered with grasslands. This planet in the Dolomar Sector has rolling hills and deep valleys across its entire surface.

The planet Kashyyyk is the homeworld of the Wookiee species. This jungle world is full of natural wonders and amazing sights. Its huge trees form an extensive ecosystem which is divided into several horizontal levels.

The planet Kessel is a world of oppression and opportunity. The oppression comes from the planet-wide Imperial correctional facility. The opportunity takes the form of one of the planet's primary products, the controlled commodity called spice.
Ketaris is a major trade center in the Dolomar Sector. Ketaris has a few large continents surrounded by great oceans.

Khomm possesses wide blue seas great land masses and an abundance of oxygen. Its inhabitants and its society have remained unchanged for a millennium since they decided that they had reached perfection. All of the citizens of Khomm are clones of previous generations.
Khuiumin was the base of operations for the notorious Eyttyrmin Batiiv pirates until the Empire made a concerted effort to destroy the group during the early days of the New Order.
Kiffex is a recently colonized planet in the Jospro sector. The planet's rich soil has the potential to turn this world into a major agriculture producing planet.
Kimanan is a forest planet in the Orus sector where the pets known as furballs come from.
Kinyen is a planet with many differing climates. The intelligent, indigenous life there are three-eyed humanoids known as Gran.

Kirdo III is desert world in the Xappyh sector that has windstorms that can reach several hundred kilometers per hour. It is covered by its white sand and pockets of dried mud. Its inhabitants, the Kitonaks, mate only once every ten years.
Kirrek, a planet of the Quelli sector, has an advanced population which survives, for the most part, off its abundant plant life.
Klatooine is a world carpeted with savannas. This world is home to an alien species known as the Klatooinians, who have two distinct tentacles coming from their heads.
Kothlis atmosphere is beneficial to most humanoids, being rich in oxygen. This world is populated by a colony of Bothans.

The small world of Kubindi, home of the unique looking Kubaz, is constantly bombarded with radiation from its primary star. The Kubaz are well known throughout the galaxy as expert cooks of unusual cuisine using the vast species of insects found on Kubindi. The Imperial spy Garindan is one of the most famous Kubaz.

Lafra, a planet situated near Corporate Sector space, is the home of a race of intelligent, gray-skinned humanoids. These beings were once flyers, though they lost this ability over eons of evolutionary change.
Lelmra is a very mountainous planet in the Churba Sector.
A planet with a diverse ecosystem in the Sumitra sector. Most of the planets regions can support humanoid life.
Mantessa is a planet encased in a treacherous jungle. What makes the wilderness so dangerous are the panthacs, predatory animals, who stalk and kill quite efficiently in their jungle home.
The grassy world of Mindar located in the remote Kanchen sector, has little to offer the galactic community other than vast landmasses that would be ideal for farming.
Moltok is an oxygen rich planet with hot rain forests covering its lower latitudes. Active volcanoes fill the skies with ash which protect the planet from the harmful effects of its sun.

Mon Calamari, also known as Calamari, is nearly completely covered with water. It is home to two separate races, the Mon Calamari and Quarren. The surface of the planet is covered with huge floating cities that house both species. The Mon Calamari had already constructed enormous organic styled starships and had begun traveling space when their planet was discovered by the Empire. The Empire planned to enslave Calamari and after meeting with resistance from its natives, destroyed three of its floating cities. After this, Calamarian starships were converted into warships and the shipyards in orbit around Calamari and its single moon became an important resource for the Alliance. Calamari is the homeworld of Admiral Ackbar.
Morvogodine is a planet that is crawling with life, with its varying conditions and ample sunlight it has produced many different species of plants and animals.
Mrisst, a planet whose many cultures had yet to develop three dimensional art when first contacted by the Old Republic. This world is midsized and can support human life.

Munto Codru is located in the Kanchen sector. The planets inhabitants, the Codru-Ji, have four arms. The animal life on this mountainous planet also consists of Codru-Ji in their infant stages, wyrwulfs and bats.
Nal Hutta, which means Glorious Jewel in Huttese, is one of the systems colonized by the Hutts. The infamous Jabba the Hutt was born in the system.
Nam'ta is a gas giant in the Artivis Sector.
Neelgaimon is a hot drought ridden desert planet in the Xappyh sector. The planet's main industry is the development and maintenance of its hazardous sandmines.
Nentan is basically a barren wasteland of a planet on which the only point of interest is the ancient ruin city of an unknown alien race.

The planet New Cov is the third world in the Churba system. It has no indigenous intelligent life, but its vast jungles teem with abundant natural resources. The sentient plants of the world are a great source of biomolecules, an important manufacturing component that cannot be synthesized.
Norulac has numerous mountain ranges strewn all over its surface. Its lack of a strong government has enabled various groups avoiding authorities elsewhere to take refuge on this planet.
Located in the Calaron sector, Norval II is abounding with organic based life in its many regions.
The reputation that has developed over the centuries is that the complete knowledge of the galaxy has been gathered within the computers of Obroa-skai's information repositories.
Located in the Mayagil sector, Oetrago is covered with what can almost be objectively viewed as unattractive plant life.
Omwat is a world of grasslands and mountains. The aliens on Omwat are believed to be capable of accomplishing great mental achievements.
Mountains are the distinctive feature on this livable planet. Every land mass on the planet is dotted with mountain chains.
Ord Pardon is mostly made up of temperate zones. It has a few desert areas, but for the most part is quite comfortable for humans.
Ord Trasi is situated in the Dufilvan Sector. Conditions on the planet are favorable to a majority of the galaxy's citizens.

Orto is a small planet in the Sluis sector. The planet's cold temperature and its thin atmosphere make its surface inhospitable to most plant life. The species there known as Ortolans are not highly industrialized but are quite adept at food production.

Palanhi is a world where its land mass covers more area than its oceans do. Though most of the planet is quite stable, it has a lot of volcanic activity under its oceans. These volcanoes are continually producing new islands on the oceans surface.
The primary planet in the Panto star system is Pantolomin. The entire system operates as a resort for galactic travelers.
A planet in the Dolomar Sector that can support almost any kind of life.
Phraetiss is a world coated with thick and very tall grasslands.
Pil Diller is a forest planet in the Quelli sector. According to records this planet was home to a now extinct species of singing trees.
Due to very strong winds on Poderis there is not much plant life there. The colonists who inhabit this planet have constructed huge walls to shield their cities from the destructive winds.
Praesitlyn is a world in the Sluis sector and is covered with forests. It is also a major hub for communication between planets and sectors.
This world is densely covered with very old forests. The planet is populated by Gamorreans who colonized this world long ago.

This world is covered by a variety of different grasses. Along the planets axis the grasses grow several meters long, the rest of the planets surface is carpeted with grass from two to ten centimeters.
Rafa IV is a planet of ice. The Sharu are believed to have originated from this planet, in fact the planet is surrounded by debris that may well be one of their early attempts to get to space.
The planet Ralltiir was one of the Rebel Alliance's first supporters. When its ties and allegiances were discovered, it was subjugated by Imperial forces.
The planet Rishi is a hot, moist world of mountains, valleys, and swamps. Human and alien colonists live in the deep valleys; the native Rishii live in the high mountains.
The planet Ruuria is home to the insectiod species called Ruurians. The world's society is made up of one hundred and forty-three colonies, and every Ruurian belongs to a single colony for life.

This dry, rocky world of shadowy valleys and mist-covered peaks has a thin but breathable atmosphere. Most of this world's inhabitants live in perpetual darkness on the side of the planet that eternally faces away from its primary star. It is also the home to the Twi'lek species and of the Alliance pilot Mazer Rackus.
A mid-size planet in the Dolomar Sector that has a myriad of assorted climates.
This planet is home to the seal-like Sedrians. It is a water planet which uses living coral as a power source.
Selaggis is a planet in the Quelli sector with a favorable climate that has attracted colonists.

Located in the Corellian Sector, Selonia is home to the species known as Selonians. Selonians live in hives centered around a fertile female. The planet is for the most part covered with water, but it does have a large number of islands.
Skor II is the homeworld of the Squibs. The planet is small and completely covered with ice.
Sluis Van is the primary planet and central star system in the Sluis sector. It is the homeworld of the Sluissi.
Smarteel is a desert type planet in the Kanchen sector. The planet has its share of outlaws who call this planet their home and deal in illegal trade.
Spefik is a barren and desolate planet located in the Artivas Sector.
Spuma has a number of prairies, mountains and jungles located throughout its surface. This medium sized planet is in the Kanchen sector.

Stic is the barren world in the Xappyh sector, which is home to the insect species Xi'Dec. The planet is subject to severe climate changes, this rapidly changing environment has caused the Xi'Dec to evolve into hundreds of species to survive.
Storthus is a desolate planet that is home to the giant stone eels. This indigenous species is composed of living rock.
The star system of Sullust, with its primary planet of the same name, threw its support to the Rebel Alliance after the Rebel victory at Yavin.
Svivren is a major trading point in the galaxy. It has a pleasant atmosphere and is located in the Sesswenna sector.
Located in the Farfin Sector, Taanab has had problems with pirate raids the last few millennia. Most of the planet is sown with green meadows.

Talus shares its orbit with its sister planet Tralus, they both have the same center of gravity. Talus has a myriad of climates throughout its atmosphere that would be hospitable to a great many galactic citizens.
Tangrene has an oxygen-nitrogen atmosphere which is constantly replenished by the lush plant life on many parts of this world.

Tatooine, the primary planet in the star system of the same name, is a desert world far from the bright center of the galaxy. Twin suns beat down on this sand-covered world, burning the great expanses of desert and all who dwell there.
Thanta Zilbra is a vibrant planet in the Xappyh sector that has a small population.
This small planet has relatively huge mountain ranges. This planet, located in the Xappyh sector, has mountain peaks that are visible to ships in orbit.
Thrakia, a planet with large land masses and rich in natural resources, is home to an intelligent insect species.

This planet is completely covered by shallow oceans. The only surface features are coral reefs which protrude from the water. The ocean currents distribute warm water around the planet creating a temperate zone which covers the entire planet. Tibrin circles the yellow star Cal and has one barren moon called Plah.
The planet Tieos is completely covered by grasslands. This planet holds a lot of potential as a future agricultural planet.
Tierfon is a small planet in the Sumitra sector. The planet's surface is dotted with mountain ranges, huge gorges and rock cliffs.
Togoria orbits the star Thanos. It is covered by grassy plains and rolling hills.
Tokmia is a lush forest planet in the Churba Sector.
Little is known about the remote forested world of Toprawa. The Alliance has used it in the past as an outpost for transferring information from their network of spies to the Alliance High Command.
Tralus shares its orbit with its sister planet Talus, they both have the same center of gravity. Talus is quite hospitable to most sentient beings of the galaxy due to its many climates.
Trammis III is the home of the giganticreptiloids sometimes called dinosaurs.
A small almost lifeless planet in the Mayagil sector. Nefariously used by many in the galaxy to describe a dead thing, as dead as a Triton moon.
Trogan is located in the Jospro sector. This planet is subject to strong tidal forces which create various natural wonders on the planet's surface.

This planet is full of deadly radiation except in some places where the radiation is held in check by force fields. This was home to the Sorcerers of Tund, before the radiation when the planet was awash in life.
The planet Ukio, the primary world in the system of the same name, is an agricultural world that serves as one of the top producers of foodstuffs.
Umgul is a mist covered planet which is known galaxywide for its gambling and sports attractions. Gambling is considered so important on this world that cheating is punished by death.
Urdur is a planet that can sustain life, but it is so far from its sun that it is continually frozen.
Uvena is controlled by the species called Shistavanen Wolfmen, who evolved in its forests. The Wolfmen are often employed as scouts due to their hunting skills.
Vagran has an oxygen atmosphere and different temperate zones all varying in temperature and precipitation. Its climates and its favorable sunlight enables Vagran to support a great deal of life.
Valrar is a planet covered in dense forests. The forests form huge canopies blocking out most of the sunlight.
Varn is a water planet in the Quelli sector. The farmers and fishers who populate this planet live at its archipelagoes.
This planet is blanketed by large prairies. The species that inhabits the planet is known as the Chevin. The society on Vinsoth is nomadic and they follow herds of grazing backshin.
Vjun is a dark lifeless planet in the Moddell sector. Powerful acid rain associated with Vjun's harsh climate prevents any plant life from growing.

Vodran is a murky, humid planet that is comprised of jungles. This is the planet on which the garbage feeding dianoga evolved. Now this creature can be found in trash dumps and refuse centers throughout the galaxy.

Vortex a dull colored planet, is subject to sharp winds. The planet is home to the Vors, a race with wings but no emotion. The center of the Vor's society is The Cathedral of Winds, a building that resonates with tones as the planets strong winds strike it.
Waskiro is heavily involved in trade, namely mining. This mountainous planet is located in the Jospro sector.
Wistril, one of the planets of the Farfin Sector, not only has rainforests but deserts and savannas.
Womrik is a mountainous tourist planet in the Dufilvan Sector. The sights of its ice capped peaks are second to none and a source of pride for the planet's inhabitants.

Woostri has an atmosphere that supports organic based life. The plant life on this planet does not turn out an abundance of oxygen. This causes many on the planet to believe the planet has a thin atmosphere when in actuality there is a shortage of oxygen that their bodies are striving to supply them with.
It is located in the Dolomar Sector and can support humanoid life. Wor Tandell's mixture of color, blue, white, and brown, comes from its oceans, clouds, and land masses.
Xa Fel is a populated planet in the Kanchen sector that readily supports almost all forms of life.
Xyquine is one of the many inhabitable planets of the Corellian Sector. Xyquine has quite a diverse array of life in its many regions.

Yag'Dhul is a small barren planet in the Glythe sector. The planet has such strong tidal forces that often the planets water and atmosphere are drawn to different parts of the planet leaving the rest of the planet in a vacuum. The intelligent beings known as Yag'Dhuls can survive in a vacuum and are experts at mathematics.
Yaga Minor is a planet which is known throughout the galaxy for its major shipbuilding facilities. Since it is in the Sesswenna sector it is near the center of power for the Empire.
Yavin is a gas giant planet in an outer rim territory of the same name. The fourth moon of Yavin served as the location for a Rebel base during the Battle of Yavin.
Zebitrope IV is known for its rolling savannas as well as being the homeworld of the species who produce the drug lesai. Lesai lets the user go without sleep but is addictive.
This planet is the home of the seaweed-like aliens known as the Zeffliffl. They inhabit the shallow seas surrounding the southern continent. Zeffliffl is the fourth planet orbiting Markbee's star.

The A-wing is a lightweight, extremely quick and agile starfighter. The A-wing carries twin, wing-mounted blaster cannons, an extensive sensor package and light combat shielding which make it an ideal choice for hit and fade missions. It was crafted to be the Alliance's solution to the newer and faster TIE models.

The Dreadnaught is an ancient heavy cruiser that dates from the time of the Old Republic. The Alliance has refit many of these ancient vessels with automated controls and faster sublight engines. Many of these became what are known as Rebel Assault Frigates.

Alliance escort carriers serve a similar purpose to their Imperial counterparts. Escort carriers are capable of carrying and refueling wings of starfighters and dramatically increase the starfighter capacity of the fleet to which they are attached. Alliance carriers have better shielding than the Imperial models, but have relatively weak firepower.

The CC-9600 is the latest iteration of the CC series of capital ships. It is more compact and requires fewer crew members than the previous CC-7700. While the CC-7700 was primarily a platform for gravity well projectors, the CC-9600 is a pure combat vessel. The firepower and shielding of the CC-9600 are considerable, while allowing enough cargo space to accommodate two regiments of ground troops.

The assault frigate is one of the Rebel Alliance's main front line warships. Built using the framework of Old Republic Dreadnaughts, Alliance engineers created a ship with greater speed and maneuverability while still retaining the Dreadnaught's weapon complement.

Assault transports are designed to delivery Imperial assault troops anywhere in the galaxy quickly with little or no escort. While only capable of carrying one regiment, the Assault transport has the firepower, speed, and maneuverability to attack lightly defended systems without escort. Additionally, Assault transports are equipped with the fastest available hyperdrive systems.

The unusual looking B-wing fighter was conceived by Admiral Ackbar to be a new generation of heavy assault starfighter. Carrying proton torpedo launchers, blaster cannons and ion cannons which are mounted to its gyroscopically stabilized cockpit, the B-wing is a formidable opponent.

The aging bulk cruiser class of capital ships has existed since the time of the Old Republic. Extremely reliable and rugged, the cruiser has become one of the mainstay ships of the Alliance fleet. Sporting a reasonable amount of firepower and shielding, the cruiser is suited for most any type of operation.

The bulk transport is the life blood of intergalactic trade and commerce. The extensive cargo capacity of the transport makes it ideal for moving everything from loads of juri juice to regiments of troops.

The Carrack light cruiser is by far the fastest Imperial escort ship. Approximately 350 meters in length, it carries a crew of just over one thousand and an ample amount of firepower. It is well suited for a variety of escort and combat missions.

The Corellian Gunship was designed to be two things, fast and deadly. Engines fill more than half of its interior structure while the external hull mounts a large number of turbolaser batteries, and quad laser cannons making it an ideal anti-starfighter platform.

The CC-7700 is an older sibling to the Corellian Gunship. While lightly armed, the CC-7700 is designed primarily as a platform for a gravity well projector. The function of this vessel is to prevent the withdrawal of enemy vessels from a battle zone.

A mid-sized capital ship constructed in the Corellian shipyards, the Corvette has had a long history of service. At 150 meters long and carrying a reasonable complement of shielding and armament, it can function as a cargo transport, or front line warship. Since this vessel sees a lot of action with the Corellian pirates and the Alliance, it has been nicknamed the Blockade Runner.

The SoroSuub Liberator's slender hull disguises the fact that it can carry three troop regiments and six fighter squadrons. For a capital ship, the Liberator is diminutive of size, but still finds room to host a formidable weapons complement consisting of ion cannons and heavy turbolaser batteries. Utilizing state-of-the-art hull armor, new shielding and drive unit technology, the Liberator is one of the most advanced warships in the galaxy.

An immense battle station 120-kilometers in diameter, the Death Star was the pinnacle of Imperial engineering. Designed by Chief Engineer Bevel Lemelisk and constructed above the penal colony of Despyre, the Death Star was Grand Moff Tarkin's ultimate symbol of rule by fear of force. It carried a crew of over 750,000 and enough firepower to destroy an entire system. Tarkin demonstrated the power of his masterpiece by destroying the world of Alderaan in an attempt to persuade Princess Leia Organa to reveal the location of the Alliance base. The Death Star relies on its world destroying superlaser and vast numbers of turbolasers on its surface to defend against large scale attacks, but appears to be vulnerable to small starfighters. The Death Star was later destroyed in the Battle of Yavin. Grand Moff Tarkin died with his creation at Yavin, but it is unknown how many more Death Stars he was constructing.

This older model starship dates from the time of the Old Republic. Specifically designed as a transport ship, the Galleon is completely unarmed. Later adopted by the Empire, the Galleon now serves mainly as a troop transport.

The Dreadnaught is an ancient heavy cruiser that dates from the time of the Old Republic. Standard Imperial Dreadnaughts are basically the same as the original Old Republic Dreadnaughts except they now carry a hangar deck for a squadron of TIE fighters. Because of its relatively slow speed, the Imperial Dreadnaught primarily serves as a convoy escort or patrol ship.

Imperial escort carriers were designed to complement a fleet's starfighter capacity and provide a hyperspace capable platform for TIE class fighters to use during hyperspace transport. Escort carriers are very lightly armed and shielded, but are capable of carrying entire wings of fighters and shuttles.

The Empire's awesome, wedge-shaped, Imperial-class Star Destroyers form the core of the Imperial Navy. True marvels of starship engineering, Imperial Star Destroyers are 1,600 meters long and have over one hundred weapon emplacements for deep-space combat.
An upgraded version of the Imperial Star Destroyer which boasts a heavily reinforced hull and more powerful heavy turbolaser batteries and cannons.

The 600-meter long star cruiser is built on a standard, heavy-cruiser hull , but it carries an artificial gravity-well generator and four gravity-well projectors which, when activated, prevent nearby starships from escaping into hyperspace.

The Lancer frigate is a 250-meter long, capital combat starship that the Empire designed specifically to combat Rebel starfighters. These vessels were designed by the Empire shortly after a small number of Rebel X-wing and Y-wing fighters destroyed the Death Star battle station.

The Dauntless is the second largest cruiser in the Alliance fleet. Built from the hull of a luxury liner, it carries an excellent sensor package and countermeasure equipment. Extra shielding and armaments were added to make the Dauntless an effective front line ship.

These vessels can haul an immense amount of equipment considering that they are only 90 meters long. The medium transport's outer hull is little more than a thick shell; the interior is mainly open space. These transports are useful in transporting medicine, food, fuel, spare parts, weapons, raw materials, and troops.

The Mon Calamari cruisers were originally spaceliners which were converted into military use. This 1,200-meter long cylindrical ship looks more organic than technological, but they are considered to be among the finest vessels traveling space. Built with many redundant systems, the Mon Cal cruiser is difficult to repair and service but extremely reliable in combat situations.

The TransGalMeg Industries Bulwark class capital ship is a mobile fortress. It is one of the most battle-proof ships employed by the Rebel Alliance. Though plagued by rumors of unreliable electronics and sensors, the Bulwark's extensive arsenal, shielding and carrying capacity make it ideal for large scale operations.

The Nebulon-B frigate is 300 meters long and was originally designed for use against starfighters. This vessel has more weaponry than most ships of its class, and its capacity to carry two squadrons of fighters makes it a superior vessel.

The 300-meter long Imperial Star Galleon is designed to combine the cargo space of a bulk freighter with the armament of a combat ship. This vessel can carry cargo and defend itself, thus eliminating the need to have separate freighters and escort craft.

This 450-meter long Imperial star cruiser was designed to be massed-produced in a short amount of time. Thanks to their modular design, the cruisers' interiors can be configured to accommodate specific mission profiles.

The Super-class Star Destroyer, the newest model in the fleet, is eight kilometers long, dwarfing even the massive Imperial Star Destroyers. The Super Star Destroyer is approximately five times as powerful as its smaller predecessor. Frequently used as a command vessel, the Super Star Destroyer is the most powerful conventional warship in the Imperial inventory.

A special purpose TIE model, the TIE bomber has two pods contained between bent wing solar panels. One pod contains the pilot and gunner, and is used to control the craft. The secondary pod contains the starfighter's weapon suite, including laser cannons, and heavy assault bombs. The bomber, like other TIE models, has no hyperdrive, life support or shielding.

The TIE defender marks a departure from the normal line of Imperial fighter design. It is one of the few production fighters to be equipped with both a shield generator and hyperdrive system. The Defender mounts considerable firepower in the form of automatic laser cannons which allow it to engage multiple enemies at once. Efficient manuevering thrusters are housed in the three odd shaped solar panels which give the Defender manuverability almost on the level of the TIE Interceptor and the Alliance's A-wing. In all, the Defender is a dangerous adversary to all Alliance fighters.

TIE fighters make up the bulk of all the Imperial starfighter corps. The small craft has no life support mechanism, shield generators, or hyperdrive making them cheap to produce in large numbers. Armaments are restricted to two laser cannons mounted below the pilot's control pod.

An improvement over the original TIE design, the interceptor incorporates the bent wing solar panels and mounts four laser cannons instead of the original two. A large engine system has also been added making the interceptor as fast and manueverable as the Alliance's A-wing. However, it still lacks shield generators, hyperdrives and life support systems found on most Alliance starfighters.

The Victory Destroyer is an older star cruiser that proved to be an important part of the Imperial space fleet. This vessel was designed with planetary bombardment in mind. While a capable combat vessel, the Victory Star Destroyer can deliver accurate and devastating bombardments from orbit.

The Victory II is an upgrade of the older Victory model starship. The Victory II was designed with space combat in mind, outfitted with larger weapons and hangar bays than the original Victory. The Victory II works well as a patrol or blockade starship and maintains its predecessor's bombardment capabilities.

Designed by the Incom Corporation, the T-65 X-wing is a small, single-pilot starfighter with exceptional maneuverability. Mounting laser cannons on the tips of its four S-foils, the X-wing can bring an impressive amount of firepower against a single target. The X-wing also mounts an efficient hyperdrive and shield generator.

The older, twin-engine Y-wing still sees lots of action in the Alliance fleet. Mounting a turreted laser cannon and ion cannon, in addition to heavier armor, the Y-wing a good choice for heavy assault missions.
An Advanced Construction Yard can produce new facilities in half the time of a standard construction yard.
An Advanced Shipyard is an advanced form of shipyard which builds ships in half the time of a standard shipyard.
An Advance Training Facility trains new troops in half the time of a standard training facility.

Construction yards convert refined materials into facilities. Construction yards are used to build all types of facilities on any system controlled by your side. Construction yards are deployed on systems. In order to deploy a construction yard on a system, there must be at least one free energy point there.

The Death Star Shield is a planetary base shield generator designed to provide a protective shield around the Death Star while it is present in the system. Immense power generators and a projection dish antenna create a bubble of nearly impenetrable energy around the Death Star. While operational, nothing can directly harm the Death Star.
GenCores are responsible for generating planetary shields to protect the system from orbital bombardment. Type I GenCores are an excellent choice for planetary defense, but are expensive to maintain.

GenCores are responsible for generating planetary shields to protect the system from orbital bombardment. Type II GenCores provide 50% more shielding power than the older Type I's and are cheaper to maintain.

The KDY v-150 is a system based ion cannon used to fire on ships which are bombarding the system on which the KDY is deployed. As with all ion cannons, the KDY does no physical damage. It drains energy from the target vessel, reducing its shields and rendering it unable to fire its weapons. This is especially useful when attempting to pass units through a enemy blockade.

The LNR series of planetary based turbolaser batteries provide exceptional firepower to protect systems from unwanted guests. The LNR-I is a single cannon turbolaser capable of damaging even the most heavily armored starships.

Like the LNR-I, the LNR-II is an exceptional planetary based turbolaser battery. The LNR-II increases the firepower of the Series I by the addition of another cannon. However, some external armoring had to be sacrificed to make room for the additional cannon and power generators.

Mines are used to extract raw materials from systems. In order to deploy a mine on a system, the system must have one unused energy point and one unused raw material point. Mines work together with refineries to produce maintenance points. Maintenance points are required to provide the materials and parts required to keep units in the field.

Shipyards convert refined material points into ships and fighters. Shipyards are deployed at systems. In order to deploy a shipyard to a system, there must be at least one free energy point at the target system.

Refineries convert the raw materials extracted by mines into refined material. Refined material is used by manufacturing facilities (such as shipyards) to build ships and facilities, and to equip troops. Refineries are deployed on systems. There must be at least one unused energy point at a system before a refinery can be deployed there. Refineries work together with mines to produce maintenance points. Maintenance points are required to provide the materials and parts required to keep units in the field.

Training facilities train new trooper regiments and SpecForces by converting refined materials into the equipment used by the new trooper regiments. In order to deploy a training facility at a system, there must be at least one unused energy point at that system.
Army regiments make up the bulk of Alliance ground troops. While not as armored or well armed as Fleet regiments, Army regiments are quick to build and cheap to maintain.
Alliance fleet regiments are the Alliance's main capital ship support troops. Usually stationed aboard capital ships, fleet regiments handle everything from ship security to planetary assaults.

Bothan spies are a wondrous source of intelligence information for the Alliance. Bothans are experts at finding and extracting vital information from sources unknown. Joining the Alliance after the Battle of Yavin, the Bothan spy network has become an essential component of the Alliance's successes against the Empire.
Legendary bounty hunters are an uncontrollable force used by the Empire to locate and acquire notorious Alliance Rebels like Han Solo.

Dark troopers have been a closely guarded secret by the Empire. They are a group of elite stormtroopers that have received a form of limited Force control training making them far deadlier than the average stormtrooper. Enhanced exo-armor and weaponry make the dark troopers a fearsome force.

Alliance guerrilla cells have been the nemesis of many Imperial governors, spreading insurrection and revolt wherever they go. The Alliance has effectively used guerrilla troops to start uprisings on many Imperial-controlled worlds. Guerrillas can also be used to suppress unrest on planets controlled by the Alliance.
Army regiments comprise the largest percentage of Imperial ground troops. Army regiments are armored the same as fleet regiments, but do not carry the firepower of their fleet counterparts.

Imperial commandos are elite special forces whose missions usually entail the infiltration and sabotage of enemy troops and equipment. Commandos can also be utilized to instill unrest on enemy systems, or to suppress insurrection on Imperial-controlled worlds.

Imperial espionage droids are designed to look like any other protocol droid, except they have special transmitters built into them. These transmitters allow for communication of information back to the Empire.
Imperial fleet regiments are the mainstay troops aboard capital ships. They assist in conducting ground assault missions and are generally better equipped than regular army regiments.

Probe droids are designed as reconnaissance and information-gathering units by the Empire. Each probe droid is contained in a small hyperspace-capable pod allowing them to travel the galaxy without the need for deployment from capital ships.

Infiltrators are the Alliance's elite commando forces. They are able to enter and sabotage even the most heavily guarded Imperial installations. Infiltrators can also be utilized to abduct enemy personnel or rescue captured Alliance personnel.

Longprobe Y-wing recon teams are used by the Alliance for scouting unknown systems. Longprobe Y-wings have sacrificed their weapon loads in favor of extended fuel tanks and improved hyperdrive systems.

The Alliance Mon Calamari regiments have the highest defense and bombardment ratings of any troop regiment in the Alliance or the Empire. This makes them the ideal garrison for holding important systems.

Noghri death commandos are experts in infiltration and assassination. All Noghri have an extreme loyalty to Lord Vader and will do whatever he commands, including the abduction of enemy personnel or rescue of captured Imperial personnel.

Imperial stormtrooper regiments are the main shock troops of the Empire. Having a good balance in attack and defense strengths make them ideal for situations from planetary assault to garrison troops.

Sullustan regiments have been used by the Alliance primarily as garrison troops because of their low maintenance costs and weak attack strength. Sullustan troops are excellent at detecting and foiling covert operations on systems where they are based.

Imperial war droids are the ultimate in heavy assault troops. These berserker droids basically exterminate everything in sight. Due to their weak defensive capabilities, war droids should be used in planetary assaults, but not as garrison troops.
Wookiee regiments are the heavy hitters of the Alliance's ground troops. Their strong attack and defense strengths make them a fierce opponent even for the most fortified stormtrooper regiment.

Admiral Ackbar is one of the senior military advisors in the Rebel Alliance. While serving as a slave to Grand Moff Tarkin, Ackbar studied Imperial tactics and defenses. Now freed, Ackbar utilizes that knowledge for the Alliance.

During the time of the Old Republic, Adar Tallon was a brilliant military strategist. Many of the space combat techniques developed by Tallon are still being utilized by both the Empire and the Alliance. After many years of hiding from the Empire on Tatooine, Tallon has recently joined the Alliance.
Captain Afyon is the commander of an Alliance escort frigate, which has lately been relegated to cargo transport duties.
Bane Nothos is the commander in charge of the Imperial Outer Rim Territories patrol fleet.
Chief Engineer Bevel Lemelisk is the chief Imperial space station designer and was the creator of the Death Star.
Bin Essada is the Imperial military governor presiding over the Circarpous Major system. It is suspected that while he looks human, his origins may not be so.

Borsk Fey'lya is the leader of a large portion of the Bothan population. He is a master diplomat and was responsible for the Bothans joining the Alliance after the Battle of Yavin. Like most Bothans, Fey'lya believes the game of politics and power is more important than individual issues, and is steadily working his way up through the Alliance's command structure.

Captain Brandei is the commander of an Imperial Star Destroyer assigned to Admiral Thrawn's personal armada. Brandei's philosophy is that it is more important to live and fight another day than to die spectacularly.
One of the executive members of the Rebel Alliance's command staff, Major Derlin was assigned to locating sites for the Alliance's base after the Battle of Yavin.
General Carlist Rieekan is the commander of all Alliance ground and fleet forces in the Hoth star system.

Chewbacca is a two-meter-tall Wookiee from the world of Kashyyyk. Once the Empire conquered Kashyyyk, it declared Wookiees to be a slave species and Chewbacca was sold into slavery. He was eventually freed by a young Imperial officer named Han Solo, who sacrificed his career to save the Wookiee. Chewbacca felt a life debt was owed to Solo and has been his constant companion ever since. Together, they embarked on a life of smuggling and questionable activities for various crime lords including Jabba the Hutt. Chewbacca is also first mate on the freighter Millennium Falcon and keeps it in working order.
Major Covell is second in command of General Veer's elite Imperial ground forces.

Crix Madine was an Imperial officer in command of an elite commando force before defecting to the Alliance after the Battle of Yavin. He is currently in command of all Alliance ground forces and strategy.

Daala is the only known female Admiral in the Imperial Navy and one of the most brilliant military strategists in the Empire. Daala is currently in command of the late Grand Moff Tarkin's special weapons development task force.

Encased in imposing black armor and death's head mask, Darth Vader, Dark Lord of the Sith, is the most visible symbol of the Empire and its New Order. Vader is a powerful dark Jedi and primarily responsible for the almost complete extermination of the Order of the Jedi Knights. Once a student of Obi-Wan Kenobi, Vader was corrupted by the dark side of the Force and joined the Empire. He is a brilliant military tactician and excellent starfighter pilot. Known for his ruthlessness, few who fail Lord Vader ever live to regret their mistake. The Alliance hoped that Vader was lost during the destruction of the Death Star, but he has recently returned in command of one of the new Super Star Destroyers.

Captain Dorja is in command of an Imperial Star Destroyer. He is extremely cautious and is usually unwilling to engage the enemy in direct combat. Because of his cautious tactics, his ship has not received any casualties during the war while it has been under his command.

Admiral Drayson is a high ranking officer in the Alliance military, but unlike many others, he did not begin his career in the Old Republic or Imperial Navy. He was Admiral of the Chandrila Defense Fleet prior to conquest by the Empire. Mon Mothma, also of Chandrila, asked Drayson to join the Alliance and serve as the commander of the Alliance Headquarters ship.

Although aged and decrepit looking, Emperor Palpatine is the ultimate power in the Empire. Palpatine formed the Empire and declared himself Emperor when the Old Republic Senate fell. Since that time, the Empire under Palpatine's direction has virtually conquered the known galaxy. A true master in the dark side of the Force, Palpatine was responsible for Darth Vader's instruction in the dark side. The true extent of his powers is as yet unknown.

Garindan is one of the most famous Kubaz informants in the galaxy. Usually found lurking around the major space ports like Mos Eisley, Garindan has been responsible for exposing several Alliance covert operations to the Empire.

Garm Bel Iblis was a senator from the Corellian system and was one of the founding members of the Alliance. Due to conflicts with Mon Mothma and death warrants from the Empire, Bel Iblis has gone into hiding. He surfaces from time to time with small groups of resistance fighters before disappearing again.
Captain-Supervisor Grammel is the commander of the Imperial garrison on Circarpous V. He is well known for being ruthless and torturing prisoners whether he needed information or not.

Admiral Griff is the fleet commander who supervised the construction of the new Super Star Destroyers. He was also charged with the blockade of the Yavin system after the destruction of the Death Star.

A former smuggler for the infamous Jabba the Hutt, this scruffy-looking mercenary only recently joined the Alliance. An ace pilot, accomplished spy and leader, Solo has the ability to get into and out of dangerous situations virtually unscratched. Together with his first mate Chewbacca, Solo captains the freighter Millennium Falcon.
Captain Neva is an excellent special forces operative.

General Jan Dodonna was the Alliance officer who planned the assault on the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin. When the Alliance evacuated the base on Yavin 4, Dodonna remained behind to destroy the base and cover the retreat. Initially presumed killed at Yavin 4, Dodonna has now returned to the Alliance.
Moff Jerjerrod is the commander in charge of the construction of Imperial super weapons like the Death Star.

Commander Andrimetrum joined the Alliance after her husband was killed by Imperial forces during the occupation of her homeworld. She is an exceptional combat leader and her troops are almost fanatically loyal.
Commander Klev is an able-bodied commander who has extensive experience in both fleet and ground assault command.

Labansat is one of the most famous Weequay mercenaries. Known for his fierce brutality and deadly fighting skill, he often plies the Lesser Plooriod Cluster and Karstaxon Regions working for whomever will meet his price.

This former smuggler and pirate turned legitimate businessman has currently set up shop as Baron Administrator of Cloud City in the Bespin system. His background makes him an important and valuable ally for the Alliance. In addition to his piloting and espionage skills from his past, Lando now possesses knowledge relating to planetary upkeep and facility improvement.

Adopted daughter of the Old Republic Senator Bail Organa of Alderaan and a senator herself until the disbanding of the Imperial Senate, Princess Leia Organa is a fiery visionary and an unequaled diplomat. She is one of the most passionate voices of dissent against the Emperor's New Order. It was her bravery that provided the Alliance with the technical data they used to destroy the first Death Star.

A farm boy raised on Tatooine, Luke is a student of the Force and a Jedi in training. A skilled and daring pilot, Skywalker was responsible for the destruction of the first Death Star. His developing abilities with the Force make him an invaluable asset to the Rebellion.
Lieutenant Ma'w'shiye is a marksman on the Alliance's elite SpecForce Eclipse Team. Ma'w'shiye has undergone intense survival training and can survive on almost any kind of world.
Mazer Rackus is a Twi'lek pilot for the Alliance. He has been flying for the Alliance since crashing on an Alliance held world when he was young.

Like most Rodians, Menndo began his career as a bounty hunter, but later learned that assassination and espionage were more profitable and less hassling than bounties. An expert assassin, Menndo has been linked to the deaths of several high ranking political officials.

A former Senator in the Old Republic, Mon Mothma is the leader of the Rebel Alliance and an old foe of Emperor Palpatine. An accomplished diplomat and skilled negotiator, she is the beacon around which much of the support for the Rebellion gathers.
Commander Narra is an old hand at disrupting Imperial business. He participated in the attack on the first Death Star.
An able fleet commander in the service of Darth Vader, Captain Needa commands an Imperial Star Destroyer.
A tactless mercenary in the service of the Empire, Niles Ferrier is a smuggler, spy and inveterate war profiteer.
Noval Garaint is a bounty hunter who is never seen outside of his battle armor. He is rumored to be the real power behind Horch, the crime lord of Kheedar.
The Imperial commander at Daluuj, Commander Orlok is the Empire's facility specialist.
Orrimaarko is the leader of the Alliance resistance cell on Dressel. He is an expert in espionage and covert warfare and the first Dresselian to be given command of an Alliance SpecForce team.

Admiral Ozzel is the commanding officer of Lord Vader's personal Super Star Destroyer. Since Lord Vader seems to have a mild dislike for the Admiral, it remains to be seen how long he will retain command and his life.
An invaluable special forces man, Lieutenant Page assists the Alliance with covert operations.
Captain Gilad Pellaeon is a competent, if somewhat cautious, battle commander.
Captain Piett is first officer aboard Lord Vader's Super Star Destroyer.
A loyal and dependable servant of the Empire, Commander Pter Thanas is nevertheless among the kindest of those who follow the will of Emperor Palpatine.

Roget Jiriss is a smuggler with a sense of honor. When Jiriss agrees to deliver a shipment, it will always arrive on time. Having contacts deep within several large crime syndicates, Jiriss is also an excellent source of information for the Alliance.
Captain Virgilio commands one of the Alliance Home Guard's escort vessels.
Formerly the Emperor's right hand man and a distinguished military commander, Screed can be trusted with nearly any space battle.

Shenir Rix is a Calamari female reputed to be one of the best information dealers in the galaxy. She only deals in solid, reliable information less than 24 hours old. If the information comes from Rix, you can be guaranteed it will be accurate.
Sullustan Captain Syub Snunb commands an Alliance frigate.

A multi-talented mercenary with a sense of humor, Karrde is an inscrutable negotiator and a good man in a fight. His payroll currently flows from the Alliance, though he has been known to perform services for the Empire in the past.
The only non-human Admiral in the service of the Empire, Thrawn is a master tactician and a cool-headed warrior. His familiarity with spacecraft and cunning make him a deadly foe.
Lieutenant Tura Raftican was once an investigative reporter for the HoloNews Network. Since joining the Alliance, she has used her contacts to provide information to the Rebels.
Commander Vanden Willard serves the Alliance under General Dodonna. He is currently assigned to planetary defense at Yavin.

General Veers commands an elite group of Imperial ground assault forces currently attached to Lord Vader's command. Renowned for his brilliant strategic planning and technique, Veers is positioned to take command of all Imperial ground forces, providing he survives his time with Lord Vader.

Villar is a typical Devaronian male, filled with wanderlust and a desire to explore the galaxy. Exploration costs money, so Villar sells the information he gathers on his travels to the highest bidder, which is usually the Empire.

A veteran pilot and expert ship mechanic, Wedge Antilles is one of the few pilots who survived the attack that destroyed the first Death Star. After the destruction of the Death Star, Wedge was given command of his own squadron of fighters. He also invests a great deal of time in creating or enhancing new ship designs.
Pilot of an Imperial strike cruiser, Commodore Zuggs furthers the military aims of the Emperor.

Abduction missions are used to capture a specific character. The target of this mission must be an enemy character on a specific enemy fleet or system. The success of this mission depends on the combat attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

Abduction missions are used to capture a specific character. The target of this mission must be an enemy character on a specific enemy fleet or system. The success of this mission depends on the combat attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

The assassination mission is restricted to the Empire only and used to injure or kill an Alliance character. The target of this mission must be a single Alliance character in a specific enemy fleet or system. The success of this mission depends on the combat attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

Diplomacy missions increase loyalty for your side in a planetary system. The success of this mission depends on the diplomacy attribute of the characters and the current loyalty of the population. Diplomacy missions can only be targeted at friendly or explored neutral systems.

Diplomacy missions increase loyalty for your side in a planetary system. The success of this mission depends on the diplomacy attribute of the characters and the current loyalty of the population. Diplomacy missions can only be targeted at friendly or explored neutral systems.

Espionage missions gather information about your opponent's planetary systems, military forces and personnel. The objective of this mission must be an enemy or neutral controlled system. The success of this mission depends on the espionage attributes of the characters assigned to the mission team.

Espionage missions gather information about your opponent's planetary systems, military forces and personnel. The objective of this mission must be an enemy or neutral controlled system. The success of this mission depends on the espionage attributes of the characters assigned to the mission team.

The objective of the incite uprising mission is to create or assist in a revolt on an enemy controlled system. The success of this mission depends on the leadership attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team and the loyalty of the system.

The objective of the incite uprising mission is to create or assist in a revolt on an enemy controlled system. The success of this mission depends on the leadership attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team and the loyalty of the system.

Jedi training missions are used to enhance the abilities of Force using characters. Whenever Darth Vader or Luke Skywalker is placed together with other Force using characters, a Jedi Training mission can be formed. At the conclusion of the mission, the character or characters may have increased their Force abilities. If the system on which the training is occurring suffers a planetary bombardment, or planetary assault, or control passes over to the enemy, the training mission is considered foiled.

Jedi training missions are used to enhance the abilities of Force using characters. Whenever Darth Vader or Luke Skywalker is placed together with other Force using characters, a Jedi Training mission can be formed. At the conclusion of the mission, the character or characters may have increased their Force abilities. If the system on which the training is occurring suffers a planetary bombardment, or planetary assault, or control passes over to the enemy, the training mission is considered foiled.

Reconnaissance missions give the player information about a system. The target of this mission is a system not currently controlled by the player. This mission reveals if the system is controlled by the opponent, the facilities located on the system, the fleets and ships in the system and the trooper regiments on the system. Reconnaissance missions may only be conducted by certain SpecForces units and not by characters.

Reconnaissance missions give the player information about a system. The target of this mission is a system not currently controlled by the player. This mission reveals if the system is controlled by the opponent, the facilities located on the system, the fleets and ships in the system and the trooper regiments on the system. Reconnaissance missions may only be conducted by certain SpecForces units and not by characters.
A recruitment mission is used to recruit minor characters to a player's side. Any major character can be placed on a recruitment mission. Recruitment missions may be directed to any friendly system.
A recruitment mission is used to recruit minor characters to a player's side. Any major character can be placed on a recruitment mission. Recruitment missions may be directed to any friendly system.

The rescue mission frees captured characters from the opponent's control. The target of this mission must be a character on the same enemy fleet or enemy system. The success of this mission depends upon the espionage attribute of the mission leader, the average combat attributes of the characters and the SpecForces involved in the mission, the presence of opposing characters, and the number of opposing trooper regiments.

The rescue mission frees captured characters from the opponent's control. The target of this mission must be a character on the same enemy fleet or enemy system. The success of this mission depends upon the espionage attribute of the mission leader, the average combat attributes of the characters and the SpecForces involved in the mission, the presence of opposing characters, and the number of opposing trooper regiments.

The ship design R&D mission causes a character to contribute to the research and development process of new ships for the player's side. The character must be sent to a system which has a shipyard and the character must be capable of conducting ship design research. The success of this mission depends on the attributes of the character involved.

The ship design R&D mission causes a character to contribute to the research and development process of new ships for the player's side. The character must be sent to a system which has a shipyard and the character must be capable of conducting ship design research. The success of this mission depends on the attributes of the character involved.

The facility design R&D mission causes a character to contribute to the research and development process of new facilities for the player's side. The character must be sent to a system which has a construction yard and the character must be capable of conducting facility design research. The success of this mission depends on the attributes of the character involved.

The troop training R&D mission causes a character to contribute to the research and development process of new troop types for the player's side. The character must be sent to a system which has a troop training facility and the character must be capable of conducting troop training research. The success of this mission depends on the attributes of the character involved.

The troop training R&D mission causes a character to contribute to the research and development process of new troop types for the player's side. The character must be sent to a system which has a troop training facility and the character must be capable of conducting troop training research. The success of this mission depends on the attributes of the character involved.

The Death Star sabotage mission is restricted to the Alliance and the location of the targeted Death Star must be known. The success of this mission depends on the espionage and combat attributes of the characters and SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

A sabotage mission destroys a facility, capital ship or an object on a planetary system. The target of this mission must be a single facility, capital ship or other unit on an enemy or neutral system. The success of this mission depends on the espionage and combat attributes of the characters, and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

A sabotage mission destroys a facility, capital ship or an object on a planetary system. The target of this mission must be a single facility, capital ship or other unit on an enemy or neutral system. The success of this mission depends on the espionage and combat attributes of the characters, and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team.

This mission can only be used on a system in a state of uprising. Characters and SpecForces on this mission are used to reduce the effects of an uprising. The success of this mission depends on the leadership attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team and the current loyalty of the population.

This mission can only be used on a system in a state of uprising. Characters and SpecForces on this mission are used to reduce the effects of an uprising. The success of this mission depends on the leadership attributes of the characters and the SpecForces assigned to the mission team and the current loyalty of the population.
